title: CNI is trying to use iptables while it should use nftables (centos8)
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

podmanpod is trying to manipulate my firewall via iptables, but I'm using nftables (without firewalld) instead. I've also tried changing my `87-podman-bridge.conflist` to nftables, but that doesn't seem to do anything.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install CentOS 8.1 with podman 1.8.0 - **without** firewalld. 
2. I also have iptables installed, but off...
3. Manipulate chains etc manually.
4. Start a test container

**Describe the results you received:**

```
INFO[0000] About to add CNI network lo (type=loopback)
INFO[0000] Got pod network &{Name:upbeat_ptolemy Namespace:upbeat_ptolemy ID:2b32261e2e1032b83161f9ed2bb420ee64fd956fb1195405f31d01b7747e3dbb NetNS:/var/run/netns/cni-4706f631-a508-52bc-0d70-1ebe210c9876 Networks:[] RuntimeConfig:map[podman:{IP: MAC: PortMappings:[] Bandwidth:<nil> IpRanges:[]}]}
INFO[0000] About to add CNI network podman (type=bridge)
ERRO[0000] Error adding network: failed to list chains: running [/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status 1: iptables v1.8.2 (nf_tables): table `nat' is incompatible, use 'nft' tool.

ERRO[0000] Error while adding pod to CNI network "podman": failed to list chains: running [/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status 1: iptables v1.8.2 (nf_tables): table `nat' is incompatible, use 'nft' tool.

DEBU[0000] unmounted container "2b32261e2e1032b83161f9ed2bb420ee64fd956fb1195405f31d01b7747e3dbb"

DEBU[0000] ExitCode msg: "error configuring network namespace for container 2b32261e2e1032b83161f9ed2bb420ee64fd956fb1195405f31d01b7747e3dbb: failed to list chains: running [/sbin/iptables -t nat -s --wait]: exit status 1: iptables v1.8.2 (nf_tables): table `nat' is incompatible, use 'nft' tool.\n\n"
ERRO[0000] error configuring network namespace for container 2b32261e2e1032b83161f9ed2bb420ee64fd956fb1195405f31d01b7747e3dbb: failed to list chains: running [/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status 1: iptables v1.8.2 (nf_tables): table `nat' is incompatible, use 'nft' tool.
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

```

---


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Turns out that if I flush nftables things "just work", but when I load my firewall rules it breaks. Do I need specific chains or something? The only table that I have rules in is `inet filter`...

```
0:root@cs [/home/sjoerd]# nft list tables
table inet filter
table ip nat
table ip filter
table ip6 nat

0:root@cs [/home/sjoerd]# nft list table inet filter -n
table inet filter {
	chain INPUT {
		type filter hook input priority 0; policy accept;
		iif "lo" accept
		ct state established,related accept
		ip6 nexthdr ipv6-icmp icmpv6 type echo-request limit rate 2/second accept
		ip6 nexthdr ipv6-icmp icmpv6 type echo-request counter packets 0 bytes 0 drop
		ip6 nexthdr ipv6-icmp icmpv6 type { destination-unreachable, packet-too-big, time-exceeded, parameter-problem, echo-reply, nd-router-advert, nd-neighbor-solicit, nd-neighbor-advert } accept
		ip protocol icmp icmp type echo-request limit rate 2/second accept
		ip protocol icmp icmp type echo-request counter packets 0 bytes 0 drop
		tcp dport ssh limit rate 15/minute accept
		tcp dport { http, https } accept
		tcp dport smtp accept
		tcp dport urd accept
		tcp dport submission accept
		tcp dport imaps accept
		counter packets 276140 bytes 21840599 drop
	}
}
0:root@cs [/home/sjoerd]# podman run -it --rm alpine /bin/ash
ERRO[0000] Error adding network: failed to list chains: running [/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status 1: iptables v1.8.2 (nf_tables): table `nat' is incompatible, use 'nft' tool.

ERRO[0000] Error while adding pod to CNI network "podman": failed to list chains: running [/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status 1: iptables v1.8.2 (nf_tables): table `nat' is incompatible, use 'nft' tool.

Error: error configuring network namespace for container 3a748f8984b26dd0bec7111716c243470f0b686858992debf5e5f7b326f60328: failed to list chains: running [/sbin/iptables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status 1: iptables v1.8.2 (nf_tables): table `nat' is incompatible, use 'nft' tool.


126:root@cs [/home/sjoerd]# nft flush ruleset

0:root@cs [/home/sjoerd]# podman run -it --rm alpine /bin/ash
/ # uname -a
Linux bfcd0e25a83f 4.18.0-147.5.1.el8_1.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Feb 5 02:00:39 UTC 2020 x86_64 Linux
/ # #                                                                                                                   0:root@cs [/home/sjoerd]#
```

+1
Unable to connect to network from container using root on CentOS 8.1
Inside container, I can ping a ip address, but can't do anything else, even curl a ip address not working.
But if I don't use podman by root, it works on slirp4netns net mode.

I think my problem related to #5335
in the cni configuration you are using (/etc/cni/net.d/...) you need to make an adjustment.  remove the "backend" definition of the firewall plugin ... keep the plugin though.

I removed the backend, and podman shows the results correctly... However I still got the exact same error.

```
# podman network inspect podman |grep -C1 firewall
			{
				"type": "firewall"
			},
```

It turned out that there were some old nat related tables that snuck into my firewall (possibly by CNI). After deleting those, leaving only my own rules, it looks like it's all back in working order!

@soostdijck , @g574, @edsantiago , fyi, I just finished working on beta version of CNI plugins compatible with `nftables` and running with CentOS 8. I would greatly appreciate feedback and testing. https://github.com/greenpau/cni-plugins#table-of-contents

@greenpau Is cdoorrection available only in GitHub? Is it planned for release? RedHat has pushed CentOS8 to CentOS8.2 This release  has no iptables working. It returns exit code 4 with proposal use another utilities which doesn't exists too.
Meanwhile, Microsoft has started to work on the kernel update to ver 5.4. I'm trying to use Podman on CentOS8 distro and for me it is blocking regression, not a feature request.
In Docker I can install, enable, disable network drivers as plugin from the Docker image repositories, i.e. re-install new driver version without full upgrade.
To have bridge and bridge-nft drivers is better than build CNI from the  scratch after each correction.

The same issue exists in Podman 2.0.6 running on Ubuntu 20.10 Groovy WSL distro. iptables are forcibly replaced with nftables maybe in all contemporary Linux distro. Please submit a correction to Ubuntu  20.10 Groovy apt repository.
Microsoft and Ubuntu are stopping to support "aging" Ubuntu 20.04 LTS. Although there is no  "official" Groovy VM image I made it via manual update of Podman with dependencies but at the end facing the same problem. Contemporary Linux distro have only nftables.
